Question: Habitat is the place where an organism lives.
Desert is a hot and dry habitat.
Fish can live in freshwater and saltwater.
Camel survives in desert without much water.
Polar bear lives in cold regions.
Plants in desert have thick leaves.
Plants in aquatic habitat float on water.
Humans live in urban and rural habitats.
Frogs live both in water and on land.
Snow leopard lives at high altitudes.
Animals cannot produce their own food.
Some organisms depend on others for food.
Some plants have thorns for protection.
